data.title: Argan Stock Surges After Surprising Q4 Results
data.description: Argan Inc (NYSE: AGX) shares experienced a surge on Friday following the company’s impressive fourth‑quarter report that exceeded Wall Street’s expectations. The stock is currently facing critical resistance. AGX reached a new high due to strong financial performance, with fourth‑quarter earnings reaching $3.47 per diluted share, surpassing the predicted $1.96 and marking an increase from $2.22 in the previous year. Revenue for the quarter was $262.1 million, outperforming the anticipated $255.3 million and showing a 12.7% year‑over‑year growth. The fiscal year 2026 results revealed a net income of $137.8 million, translating to $9.74 per diluted share, compared to $85.5 million and $6.15 per share in fiscal 2025. Full‑year revenue saw an 8.1% increase to $944.6 million. The company showcased strong margins and effective project execution in the fourth quarter.

data.title: KRISPY KREME® Launches Artemis II Doughnut to Celebrate NASA's Historic Deep Space Crewed Mission, Available March 31 – April 2
data.description: In Charlotte, North Carolina, Krispy Kreme® doughnuts is making strides in celebrating NASA's upcoming launch of Artemis II - the first crewed test flight aiming for a long-term lunar presence. Krispy Kreme® will be releasing a special limited-edition Artemis II Doughnut to mark this important event in space exploration. The doughnut will be available from March 31 to April 2 at select Krispy Kreme® locations.

data.title: Tennessee Youth Suicide Remains a Pressing Concern Despite Slight Decline in 2024
data.description: In Hendersonville, Tennessee on March 27, 2026, The Jason Foundation, Inc. addressed the latest data from the CDC for 2024, indicating that suicide continues to be a significant cause of death for youths in the state. The report highlights that there has been a slight decline in the numbers of suicides among children, teenagers, and young adults.

data.title: Artelo Biosciences Announces $11.0 Million Private Placement Priced At-the-Market Under Nasdaq Rules
data.description: Artelo Biosciences, Inc. announced on March 27, 2026, that it has finalized agreements for the sale of 3,188,407 shares of common stock (or pre-funded warrant) and warrants to buy up to 6,376,814 shares of common stock, at a price of $3.45 per share, in a private placement conducted under Nasdaq rules. The warrants will carry an exercise price of $3.20 per share, become exercisable upon issuance, and expire five and a half years from the Effectiveness Date. The private placement, expected to close around March 30, 2026, is subject to standard closing conditions.

data.title: Bitcoin Plunges 3% And ETFs Bleed $171M After Ukraine Destroys Russian Oil Ports
data.description: Bitcoin (BTC) saw a 3% decrease in value following Ukraine drone strikes that led to a 40% reduction in Russia's oil export capacity. This coincided with a significant $171 million in ETF outflows, marking the largest single-day withdrawal in three weeks. President Donald Trump had previously removed sanctions on Russian crude to offset disruptions in oil supply due to the Iran war and stabilize energy markets. However, Ukraine's recent drone attacks on Russian ports and refiners in the Leningrad region undermined this strategy. Experts noted that this damage posed the most serious threat to Russia's oil exports since Putin's invasion of Ukraine in 2022. As a result, approximately 40% of Russia's oil export capacity is currently offline, creating logistical challenges and supply constraints, according to Oilprice.com. The situation unfolds amidst ongoing conflicts in the Middle East, contributing to a complex energy landscape.

data.title: Sun Pharma Highlights Advances in Alopecia Areata, Psoriasis, and Acne Research at 2026 AAD Annual Meeting
data.description: Sun Pharmaceutical Industries Limited showcased its superior position in dermatology and immunology at data presentations in Mumbai, India and Princeton, N.J. on March 27, 2026. The company, listed as SUN.BO on Reuters, SUNP IN on Bloomberg, and trading as SUNPHARMA on the NSE and BSE under ticker symbol 524715, highlighted its expertise in these fields. The presentations highlighted the company's leadership in the categories, demonstrating its commitment to innovation and excellence.

data.title: Bitcoin Skids, $14 Bil In Options Due; Cathie Wood Raises Cash
data.description: The price of Bitcoin has dropped with a significant amount of options expiring and causing an increase in liquidations. Cathie Wood from ARK Invest seems to be transitioning towards holding more cash. Approximately $14 billion worth of options are set to expire, leading to a rise in liquidations and contributing to the decline in Bitcoin's value. Cathie Wood, renowned for her work with ARK Invest, is notably moving towards cash holdings amidst the current market conditions.

data.title: Senate Agrees To Narrow DHS Shutdown, Fund TSA; Airline Stocks Keep Sliding
data.description: The Senate has approved funding for a portion of the Department of Homeland Security, which includes the Transportation Safety Administration. This decision aims to address the extended wait times experienced at airports due to the shutdown. The agreement is expected to provide relief for passengers and airlines struggling with delays. Additionally, airline stocks continue to decline amidst ongoing uncertainties.

data.title: AstraZeneca Zips Higher On Its Double Win In COPD Treatment
data.description: Shares of AstraZeneca surged on Friday following the announcement that the U.K.-based pharmaceutical company's chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD) drug achieved positive results in two phase III studies. The stock saw a significant increase after the news, reaching a notable high. This success in developing an effective treatment for COPD has attracted investor interest, leading to a boost in the company's market performance. The positive outcomes from these final-phase trials have propelled AstraZeneca's stock price upwards, reflecting optimism in the company's advancements in tackling respiratory disorders.

data.title: AI Builder's Earnings Blow Past Views. Stock Soars 34%.
data.description: In the latest quarter ending in January, the company announced earnings of $3.47 per share, showing a notable 56% increase compared to the previous year. Sales also experienced a significant rise of almost 13%, reaching $262 million. This strong performance led to the company surpassing market expectations. As a result, the stock price surged by an impressive 34%, reflecting investor optimism. The company's post titled "AI Builder's Earnings Blow Past Views" highlights this remarkable financial achievement.
